query: what was Concorde's maximum speed in miles per hour?
description: Speed is given in km/h and Mach; the answer in mph requires a unit conversion not present on the page. Regex-plan can extract the raw number but cannot derive mph.

direct_anthropic.query: what was Concorde's maximum speed in miles per hour?

snippets: Maximum speed: 1,354 mph (2,179 km/h, 1,177 kn)
Maximum speed: Mach 2.04 (temperature limited)
Cruise speed: 1,341 mph (2,158 km/h, 1,165 kn)
The highest temperature it could sustain over the life of the aircraft was 127 °C (261 °F), which limited the top speed to Mach 2.02.
Concorde had a maximum cruising altitude of 18,300 metres (60,000 ft) and an average cruise speed of Mach 2.02 (2,150 km/h; 1,330 mph), more than twice the speed of conventional aircraft.
Mach 2.02 (~2,154 km/h or 1,338 mph) cruising speed for optimum fuel consumption

synthesis.summary: According to the Wikipedia article on Concorde, the aircraft's maximum speed was 1,354 mph (Mach 2.04), as listed in the aircraft's performance specifications. This was a temperature-limited figure, constrained by the aluminium alloy airframe (Hiduminium R.R. 58) which could sustain a maximum nose-tip temperature of 127 °C (260 °F). The normal operating cruise speed was Mach 2.02, approximately 1,330–1,341 mph depending on altitude and conditions.
items: Maximum speed: 1,354 mph (converted from Mach 2.04 / 2,179 km/h)
synthesis.notes: The figure of 1,354 mph is the absolute maximum speed from the aircraft's technical specifications. Concorde's standard cruise speed was slightly lower at Mach 2.02 (~1,330–1,341 mph). The maximum speed was temperature-limited by the aluminium alloy airframe.

direct_byllm.query: what was Concorde's maximum speed in miles per hour?

snippets: Mach 2.02 (~2,154 km/h or 1,338 mph) cruising speed for optimum fuel consumption (supersonic drag minimum and turbojet engines are more efficient at higher speed); fuel consumption at Mach 2 (2,120 km/h; 1,320 mph) and at altitude of 60,000 feet (18,000 m) was 4,800 US gallons per hour (18,000 L/h).
Concorde had a maximum cruising altitude of 18,300 metres (60,000 ft) and an average cruise speed of Mach 2.02 (2,150 km/h; 1,330 mph), more than twice the speed of conventional aircraft.
The normal landing speed was 170 miles per hour (274 km/h).

synthesis.summary: According to the Wikipedia article on Concorde, the aircraft's cruising speed was Mach 2.02, which is approximately 1,330–1,338 mph (2,150–2,154 km/h). This was its operational maximum cruise speed. The article does not separately state a higher absolute maximum speed distinct from the cruise speed, as Mach 2.02 represented both the design cruise speed and the effective top speed limited by the aluminium airframe's heat tolerance.
items: Concorde cruise speed: Mach 2.02 (~1,338 mph / 2,154 km/h)
Concorde average transatlantic cruise speed: Mach 2.02 (1,330 mph / 2,150 km/h)
Concorde Mach 2 speed reference: 1,320 mph (2,120 km/h)
Concorde maximum cruising altitude: 60,000 ft (18,000 m)
Concorde normal landing speed: 170 mph (274 km/h)
synthesis.notes: The article gives slightly varying figures for Mach 2.02 in different places (1,330 mph, 1,338 mph, 2,150 km/h, 2,154 km/h) due to rounding. The top speed was limited to Mach 2.02 by the aluminium alloy (Hiduminium R.R. 58) airframe, which could not sustain temperatures above 127 °C. No separate absolute maximum speed beyond the cruise speed is stated in the provided HTML.
